merged_from: os_kinetics/jinwoo_epic_ascent_descent_mapping.json (EPIC S035-S056 counts preserved for overlapping odd/even rows).
Enumerated all non-excluded S*.h5 with dataset.include_condition_for_dataset(walking_only=True, levelground_only=False).
subject_condition_trial_max_index lists every included condition with trial groups for all subjects (Camargo S001-S056 + AddBiomechanics public S057+).

dataset_filters.s001_s056_condition_filter: dataset.is_walking_condition (excludes condition names containing static, dynamic, or backward)
dataset_filters.s057_plus_condition_filter: All non-metadata condition groups are included (dataset.include_condition_for_dataset). LG vs RA/RD for slopes/walks uses dataset.classify_loc_bucket (uphillrun/downhillrun/flatrun/walk_/etc.).

mapping_rule._comment: subjects (below): odd/even trial index only for ambiguous SA/SD or RA/RD conditions (see load_loc_ascent_descent_mapping). subject_condition_trial_max_index: documentation of trial counts for every loaded condition.
mapping_rule.openbiomechanics_bucket_hints: Conditions such as uphillrun_*, downhillrun_*, flatrun_*, walk_* are labeled via dataset.classify_loc_bucket (RA/RD/LG); they do not need odd/even JSON rows.
mapping_rule.stair_family_ambiguous: Odd/even trial index convention for classify_loc_bucket SA/SD ambiguous.
mapping_rule.incline_ramp_family_ambiguous: Odd/even trial index convention for classify_loc_bucket RA/RD ambiguous.

note_on_capital_Stair: MeMo/Camargo-style group `Stair` is ambiguous (SA/SD) under classify_loc_bucket; odd/even mapping is applied. Confirm against lab protocol if trials are not strictly paired ascent/descent.
usage: subjects: for each listed condition with N=max trial index: odd trials ascent, even trials descent (load_loc_ascent_descent_mapping). subject_condition_trial_max_index: audit of trial_* coverage for every subject/condition the dataset loader can see at this root (not itself loaded by load_loc_ascent_descent_mapping). See dataset.classify_loc_bucket for buckets on OpenBiomechanics-style names without JSON rows.
